name: Montcabrer from Cocentaina Trail (PR-CV 37)
name_original: Ascenso al Montcabrer (PR-CV 37) — Circular desde Cocentaina
description: Hike the difficult 9.8-mile Montcabrer from Cocentaina Trail (PR-CV 37) to gain 2843 feet of elevation and enjoy panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking trails around Cocentaina are set within a valley where the Serpis river flows, surrounded by prominent mountain ranges including the Sierra de Aitana, Benicadell, and Mariola. The region features Montcabrer, one of the highest peaks, and a significant portion of its territory is part of the Serra Mariola and Serreta Natural Park. This landscape offers diverse terrain, from river valleys to challenging mountain ascents. Best hiking trails around Cocentaina The most popular hiking route is Montcabrer from Cocentaina Trail (PR-CV 37), a 9.8 miles (15.8 km) trail that takes 6 hours 26 minutes to complete. This difficult route involves significant elevation gain and offers extensive panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Racó Llobet – Montcabrer Summit loop from Balconada de Llopis, a moderate 5.8 miles (9.3 km) path. This loop provides rewarding summit views and takes approximately 3 hours 34 minutes. Local hikers also love the View of the Castle – Cocentaina Castle loop from Cocentaina, a 4.5 miles (7.2 km) trail leading through historical sites and offering views of the castle, often completed in about 2 hours 28 minutes. Hiking around Cocentaina is defined by impressive mountain ranges, natural parks, and river valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Cocentaina are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 490 reviews. More than 3200 hikers have used komoot to explore Cocentaina's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Cocentaina?
A: Cocentaina offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 25 trails documented on komoot. These routes cater to different skill levels, from moderate loops to more challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there easy hiking options for beginners or families in Cocentaina?
A: Yes, Cocentaina has options suitable for beginners and families. While many routes are moderate, there is at least one easy trail available. These trails often provide a gentler introduction to the region's natural beauty without significant elevation challenges.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Cocentaina?
A: The terrain around Cocentaina is diverse, ranging from river valleys along the Serpis to impressive mountain ranges like the Sierra de Aitana, Benicadell, and Mariola. You'll find paths through natural parks, challenging ascents to peaks like Montcabrer, and trails that wind through historical sites.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Cocentaina?
A: Absolutely. Cocentaina is home to several challenging routes, particularly those ascending to Montcabrer, one of the highest peaks in the province. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e868498455/montcabrer-from-cocentaina-trail-pr-cv-37" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montcabrer from Cocentaina Trail (PR-CV 37)&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 9.8-mile route with significant elevation gain, offering extensive panoramic views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19417391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montcabrer Summit – Ermita de San Cristóbal loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Cocentaina?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Cocentaina are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular moderate example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14661146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Racó Llobet – Montcabrer Summit loop from Balconada de Llopis&lt;/a&gt;, which offers rewarding summit views. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21940452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Castle – Cocentaina Castle loop from Cocentaina&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through historical sites.

Q: What natural landmarks or attractions can I see while hiking in Cocentaina?
A: Hiking in Cocentaina offers access to several notable natural landmarks. You can explore gorges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3081175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Cint&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2897664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toll de l’Estret&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features impressive peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1390751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montcabrer Summit&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3994612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Menejador&lt;/a&gt;, providing stunning viewpoints. Don't miss the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7728506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument Valley of the Costa Blanca&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites to explore on the trails around Cocentaina?
A: Yes, Cocentaina's trails often blend natural beauty with historical interest. You can encounter sites like the Santa Bárbara Chapel, a 13th-century chapel, or the Cava Gran de Agres, a historic snow storage facility. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21940452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Castle – Cocentaina Castle loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights the local castle, and you might also find ruins and weirs along the Riu Clariano, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5983546" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins and weir on the Riu Clariano&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Cocentaina a good destination for dog-friendly hikes?
A: While specific dog-friendly designations for each trail are not always available, the natural parks and open landscapes around Cocentaina generally welcome responsible dog owners. It's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas like the Serra Mariola and Serreta Natural Park, and to check local regulations for specific trails.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Cocentaina?
A: Cocentaina offers hiking opportunities year-round, but spring and autumn are generally considered the best times. The weather is milder, making for more comfortable conditions for both valley walks and mountain ascents. Summer can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter offers cooler temperatures and potentially snow at higher altitudes like Montcabrer.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Cocentaina?
A: The hiking routes in Cocentaina are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 490 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the rewarding panoramic views from the mountain peaks, and the opportunity to explore both natural and historical sites.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or gorges to discover in the Cocentaina area?
A: Yes, the region around Cocentaina features several impressive gorges and even a waterfall. You can explore the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3081175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Cint&lt;/a&gt; or the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2897664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toll de l’Estret&lt;/a&gt;. For a waterfall experience, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2599324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg del Salt de Planes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What mountain ranges surround Cocentaina for hiking?
A: Cocentaina is nestled among impressive mountain ranges, offering a variety of hiking environments. The most prominent include the Sierra de Aitana, Benicadell, and Mariola. A significant portion of the territory is within the Serra Mariola and Serreta Natural Park, providing extensive opportunities for exploring unspoiled nature and challenging peaks like Montcabrer.
tips: In the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park. The road ends at the ruins of Mas de Llopis. From this point, a well-marked path takes us in about 45 minutes to the top of Montcabrer.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this trail?
faqs.answer: The Montcabrer from Cocentaina Trail (PR-CV 37) is a demanding route. Hikers should plan for approximately 6 to 7 hours to complete the full circular route, depending on their pace and stops for views.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail commonly starts from the Sant Cristòfol hermitage and recreational area on the outskirts of Cocentaina. Parking is generally available in this area, which serves as a convenient trailhead for the route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Montcabrer from Cocentaina Trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and can be demanding. Initially, you'll find shady sections with abundant vegetation. As you ascend, the landscape becomes more rugged and mountainous, with a mix of tracks and trails. Be prepared for steep climbs and ensure you wear good footwear.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to hike this route are typically spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, and some natural springs may dry up. Winter can offer clear views but may present colder conditions at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the PR-CV 37 trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on this trail within the Serra de Mariola Natural Park. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety on steep or rugged sections.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for the Serra de Mariola Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or permits required to hike the Montcabrer from Cocentaina Trail (PR-CV 37) or to access the Serra de Mariola Natural Park. However, always respect park regulations regarding waste, fire, and wildlife.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several points of interest. You'll pass natural springs like Font Huit Piletes and Font de Sanxo (though they may dry up). Higher up, you'll encounter the ruins of Mas de Llopis and, of course, reach the Montcabrer Summit, which provides magnificent panoramic views extending to La Safor, the Mediterranean Sea, Benicadell, La Serrella, and Aitana on clear days. You might also spot ancient 'caves' (icehouses).

faqs.question: Is there water available on the trail?
faqs.answer: There are several natural springs along the route, such as Font Huit Piletes and Font de Sanxo. However, these springs can dry up during periods of extreme drought, especially in summer. It's always advisable to carry sufficient water for the entire hike, as relying solely on natural sources can be risky.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Montcabrer from Cocentaina Trail (PR-CV 37) shares sections with other routes. You may find parts of the GR-7 long-distance path, and it also intersects with trails like 'Montcabrer desde Cocentaina' and 'Senda d'Agres - Cocentaina'.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter in the Serra de Mariola Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The Serra de Mariola Natural Park is rich in biodiversity. Hikers often have the chance to observe a colony of griffon vultures, particularly from the Alt de les Pedreres. The park is also known for its aromatic herbs and diverse flora.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restaurants near the trailhead in Cocentaina?
faqs.answer: Cocentaina is a town with various amenities. You can find cafes, restaurants, and other services in the town center, which is a short distance from the Sant Cristòfol hermitage trailhead. It's a good place to refuel before or after your hike.
